title: pedilonum lankaviense
intro: Pedilonum lankaviense is a plant species belonging to the family Rutaceae, commonly found in Sri Lanka and other parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Pedilonum lankaviense are attributed to its essential oils, which are rich in limonene and other bioactive compounds. Studies have shown that the plant has various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Pedilonum lankaviense is often used in traditional medicine to make teas, infusions, and other remedies, which are said to promote relaxation, ease pain, and boost the immune system.
